MessageTimeline had no handler for the app regaining focus/visibility, so the scroll-to-end pinned by useLayoutEffect/ResizeObserver was never re-asserted on return (messages arriving while hidden got scrolled against stale WKWebView geometry; the 96px fuzzy threshold left any sub-threshold offset uncorrected). Added a visibilitychange + window focus listener that re-snaps to the exact bottom, only when isAtEndRef is true so intentional scroll-up is preserved. typecheck clean, 69 tests pass, build clean. Manual scroll verification in-app still recommended.

Implemented react-md-editor timeline toggle
Fixed reply reaction emoji updates by including reply metadata changes in the MessageRow memo comparator.
Implemented message deletion for timeline
Fixed attachment preview styling to hide react-doc-viewer header and remove image/checkerboard backgrounds.
Changed current-user reaction highlight from pale fill to inset outline; focused MessageTimeline test passes.
Selected reaction pill now uses only an inset outline and keeps the default pill text color; focused test and build pass.
Biome check is clean; tests and build pass; typecheck only hits the existing CSS side-effect import declaration blocker.
Fixed by disabling @uiw/react-md-editor built-in command shortcuts in NewMessageComposer and adding a regression test; full bun test passes.

Fixed composer disabled-state flicker by keeping the composer enabled during channel-history loading when a channel is selected; added ChatShell regression coverage. Verified with full bun test and build.

Fixed channel selection oscillation by preventing the startup connection effect from rerunning on config changes caused by channel selection; added regression coverage. Full bun test and build pass; typecheck remains blocked only by known CSS side-effect import TS2882 errors.
Fixed self-DM direct-channel label fallback and added regression coverage.
Sidebar now uses the shared direct-channel user resolver, so self DMs use the current user's image/status/avatar fallback.
Implemented Phase 1 foundation types, shared RPC payload types, and default WebRTC configuration.
Implemented Phase 2 WebRTC core: media device management, Mattermost call signaling, and core call manager.
Phase 3 WebRTC UI components added and targeted verification passed
Completed Phase 4 integration: CallManager provider, signaling post routing, timeline filtering, call controls/toasts, sidebar active call panel, and test wrapper updates.
Completed Phase 5 polish: desktop call notification RPC, incoming call notification trigger, user-facing call error toast, and focused verification.
